steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence joins two complete independent clauses — tan sweatpants take on the form of a camel as they dangle from a clothesline and the red handles of a pair of pliers become the thongs of flip-flops made of nails — that are parallel examples of Stentzel's technique.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — comma only: joining two independent clauses with just a comma creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — semicolon: correctly connects two closely related independent clauses of equal grammatical weight. ✓
  • Choice C — em-dash: typically signals an interruption or aside — less standard for joining two parallel examples. ✗
  • Choice D — colon: used to introduce a list or elaboration, not to join two parallel independent clauses. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
